signals.headline: Bitcoin rebounds above $70,000 with $400-500M short liquidations fueling rally | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin rebounds above $70,000 with $400-500M short liquidations fueling rally | YES | MED confidence**

Bitcoin traded at $74,535 as of April 14, up 5.34% in 24 hours and 3.7% weekly, after re-establishing above $70,000 following consolidation.[2] This surge liquidated $400-500 million in shorts, accelerating upward momentum via forced buying.[2] Polymarket shows 92% probability of BTC at $75,000
matched_markets: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 21?

signals.headline: South Korea Inflation Accelerates to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Inflation Accelerates to 2.2% Year-on-Year in March | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
Consumer price inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026, up from 2.0% in February and marking the highest reading since December[4]. Rising global oil prices were the primary driver, with transportation costs rising 5% year-on-year[1].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2.5% for 7th Consecutive Meeting | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
The Bank of Korea announced on April 10, 2026, its seventh consecutive freeze of the benchmark interest rate at 2.5%[2]. Governor Rhee Chang-yong's final rate-setting decision maintained the rate despite renewed inflationary pressures and foreign exchange instability[2].

signals.headline: Fed Rate Hike Discussions Emerge Amid Middle East Tensions | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Fed Rate Hike Discussions Emerge Amid Middle East Tensions | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
March FOMC minutes revealed that some Federal Reserve participants argued the target rate range should be raised if inflation exceeds 2%, with rising energy prices identified as a key variable[5]. However, CME FedWatch reflected a 98.4% probability of a rate hold at the April FOMC meeting[5].

signals.headline: Record Foreign Capital Outflow from South Korea | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Record Foreign Capital Outflow from South Korea | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
The Middle East war drove a record $36.55 billion foreign capital outflow from Korea, contributing to Korean won volatility and emerging market currency weakness[5].

signals.headline: New Bank of Korea Governor Nominee Announced | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
New Bank of Korea Governor Nominee Announced | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
Shin Hyun-song was nominated as the new Bank of Korea governor, with confirmation hearing responses indicating plans to strengthen financial safety nets and currency swaps[5].
